title: Create an automation that creates a date x number of days from a fixed start date
excerpt: Is it possible to create automations where a column date is added a set number of days after a fixed date.  We use Monday for member management - they start membership on a fixed date we import into Monday - we would the…

posts.author: KLloyd (Karen )
posts.cookedHtml: Is it possible to create automations where a column date is added a set number of days after a fixed date. We use Monday for member management - they start membership on a fixed date we import into Monday - we would then like to have an automation that populates 3 date columns when they are due customer service calls i.e. 15 days after starting, 30 days after starting and 60 days - I cannot for the life of me work out if this is possible!!

posts.author: techguy (Tahara)
posts.cookedHtml: Yes, it’s possible to create automations in Monday to populate date columns based on a fixed start date. Set up a custom automation to trigger 15, 30, and 60 days after the membership start date, updating the corresponding date columns with the calculated dates. This can streamline your customer service call scheduling process.

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey Karen,

Could something like this work for you?

This would require the date being set across all date columns to that membership date and then pushing those 3 date columns by the specific amount of days in line with the customer service calls.

Let me know what you think
